---
title: "Healthcare Mock Data - Descriptive Analytics & Visualization"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
# Path: run from repo root or set DATA_DIR
DATA_DIR <- if (dir.exists("data/raw")) "data/raw" else file.path("..", "..", "data", "raw")
```

## Load data

```{r load}
patients   <- read.csv(file.path(DATA_DIR, "patients.csv"))
encounters <- read.csv(file.path(DATA_DIR, "encounters.csv"))
encounters$admit_date    <- as.POSIXct(encounters$admit_date)
encounters$discharge_date <- as.POSIXct(encounters$discharge_date)
claims     <- read.csv(file.path(DATA_DIR, "claims.csv"))
readmissions <- read.csv(file.path(DATA_DIR, "readmissions.csv"))
```

## Encounter volume by type

```{r encounter-type}
tbl <- table(encounters$encounter_type)
barplot(tbl, main = "Encounters by type", col = "steelblue", las = 1)
```

## Length of stay (inpatient)

```{r los}
encounters$los_days <- as.numeric(difftime(encounters$discharge_date, encounters$admit_date, units = "days"))
inpatient <- encounters[encounters$encounter_type == "inpatient", ]
hist(inpatient$los_days, breaks = 30, main = "Length of stay (inpatient, days)", xlab = "Days", col = "steelblue")
summary(inpatient$los_days)
```

## Claims: total paid by claim type

```{r claims}
agg <- aggregate(total_paid ~ claim_type, data = claims, FUN = function(x) c(mean = mean(x), sum = sum(x), n = length(x)))
agg
boxplot(total_paid ~ claim_type, data = claims, main = "Total paid by claim type", col = "lightblue")
```

## 30-day readmissions

```{r readmissions}
n_30 <- sum(readmissions$is_30_day == 1)
n_total <- nrow(readmissions)
cat("30-day readmissions:", n_30, "of", n_total, "index discharges\n")
cat("Rate:", round(100 * n_30 / n_total, 2), "%\n")
hist(readmissions$days_to_readmit, breaks = 20, main = "Days to readmission", xlab = "Days", col = "coral")
```

## Summary

Descriptive analytics and visualizations on the healthcare mock dataset: encounter mix, LOS distribution, claim costs by type, and readmission distribution. Suitable for stakeholder reporting and as input to predictive models.
